content:
# Philippians Chapter 1 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), Quire 85, pages 194-195 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/PHP_001_page_001.jpg, PHP_001_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Paulos and Timotheos, slaves of Christ Jesus, to all the holy-ones in Christ Jesus, the [ones] being in Philippoi, together-with overseers and servants:

**2** Grace to you and peace from God [the] Father of us and [the] Lord Jesus Christ.

**3** I give-thanks to the God of me upon every remembrance of you,

**4** always in every petition of me on-behalf-of all of you, with joy the petition making,

**5** upon the partnership of you unto the good-message from the first day until the now,

**6** having-been-persuaded [of] this very-thing, that the [one] having-begun-in you a good work will-bring-it-to-completion until [the] day of Christ Jesus;

**7** just-as it-is righteous for me to think this on-behalf-of all of you, because-of the having me in the heart you, both in the bonds of me and in the defense and confirmation of the good-message, fellow-sharers of me of the grace all of you being.

**8** For [my] witness [is] the God, how I-long-for all of you in [the] bowels of Christ Jesus.

**9** And this I-pray, that the love of you yet more and more may-abound in full-knowledge and all perception,

**10** unto the you to-test the [things] differing, so-that you-may-be pure-ones and not-stumbling unto [the] day of Christ,

**11** having-been-filled [with the] fruit of righteousness, the [fruit which is] through Jesus Christ, unto [the] glory and praise of God.

**12** To-know but I-want you, brothers, that the [things] concerning me rather unto advancement of the good-message have-come,

**13** so-that the bonds of me manifest in Christ became in the whole Praitorion [praetorium] and to the remaining-ones all,

**14** and the greater-number of the brothers in [the] Lord, having-been-persuaded by the bonds of me, more-abundantly to-dare fearlessly the word of God to-speak.

**15** Some indeed even through envy and strife, some but also through good-will the Christ are-proclaiming;

**16** the-ones indeed out-of love, knowing that unto [the] defense of the good-message I-am-set,

**17** the-ones but out-of selfish-ambition the Christ are-proclaiming, not purely, supposing affliction to-raise-up [against] the bonds of me.

**18** What for? Except that in-every way, whether in-pretense or in truth, Christ is-being-proclaimed, and in this I-rejoice. But also I-will-rejoice,

**19** for I-have-known that this to-me will-turn-out unto salvation through the petition of you and [the] supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,

**20** according-to the earnest-expectation and hope of me, that in nothing I-will-be-put-to-shame, but in all boldness, as always, also now will-be-magnified Christ in the body of me, whether through life or through death.

**21** For to me the to-live [is] Christ, and the to-die [is] gain.

**22** If but the to-live in [the] flesh, this to-me [is the] fruit of work; and what I-will-choose, I-make-not-known.

**23** I-am-being-pressed-together but out-of the two, the desire having unto the to-depart and to-be with Christ, [which is] much more better;

**24** the but to-remain in the flesh [is] more-necessary on-account-of you.

**25** And this having-been-persuaded, I-have-known that I-will-remain and I-will-continue-with all of you unto the of-you advancement and joy of the faith,

**26** so-that the boast of you may-abound in Christ Jesus in me, through the my coming again toward you.

**27** Only worthily of the good-message of the Christ conduct-yourselves-as-citizens, so-that whether having-come and having-seen you, or being-absent, I-may-hear the [things] concerning you, that you-stand-firm in one spirit, with-one-soul contending-together for the faith of the good-message,

**28** and not being-frightened in nothing by the [ones] opposing; which is to-them [a] proof of destruction, but of-you of salvation, and this from God;

**29** because to you it-was-granted, the [thing] on-behalf-of Christ, not only the into him to-believe, but also the on-behalf-of him to-suffer,

**30** the same struggle having, such-as you-saw in me and now you-hear [is] in me.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Observations (Codex Sinaiticus, pages 194-195)

1. **v.1 — ΠΑΥΛΟΣ ΚΑΙ ΤΙΜΟΘΕΟΣ ΔΟΥΛΟΙ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ ΙΗΣΟΥ:** Sinaiticus reads "Christ Jesus" (ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ ΙΗΣΟΥ) in the address, not "Jesus Christ." This order is preserved throughout where attested. The Sinaiticus text uses ΔΟΥΛΟΙ ("slaves"), not ΔΟΥΛΟΙ ΘΕΟΥ or ΑΠΟΣΤΟΛΟΙ. Paul does not claim the apostle title in this letter's greeting.

2. **v.1 — ΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΟΙΣ ΚΑΙ ΔΙΑΚΟΝΟΙΣ:** Translated "overseers and servants" rather than the later ecclesiastical terms "bishops and deacons." The Greek words here carry their ordinary sense: ΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΟΣ = "one who watches over," ΔΙΑΚΟΝΟΣ = "one who serves."

3. **v.6 — ΑΧΡΙ ΗΜΕΡΑΣ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ ΙΗΣΟΥ:** Sinaiticus reads "Christ Jesus" here. Some later manuscripts read simply "Jesus Christ." The Sinaiticus reading is preserved.

4. **v.7 — ΕΝ ΤΩ ΕΧΕΙΝ ΜΕ ΕΝ ΤΗ ΚΑΡΔΙΑ ΥΜΑΣ:** The syntax is ambiguous: it could be "because I have you in the heart" or "because you have me in the heart." The Greek word order as it stands in the manuscript (ΕΝ ΤΗ ΚΑΡΔΙΑ ΥΜΑΣ) is preserved literally, allowing the ambiguity to remain. The accusative ΥΜΑΣ most likely functions as the object, yielding "I have you in the heart," but the construction permits either reading.

5. **v.8 — ΕΝ ΣΠΛΑΓΧΝΟΙΣ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ ΙΗΣΟΥ:** ΣΠΛΑΓΧΝΑ literally = "bowels" or "entrails," the seat of deep emotion in Greek thought. I retain "bowels" for literal fidelity.

6. **v.11 — ΚΑΡΠΟΝ ΔΙΚΑΙΟΣΥΝΗΣ:** Sinaiticus reads the singular ΚΑΡΠΟΝ ("fruit"), not the plural. This is consistent with Galatians 5:22 usage.

7. **v.13 — ΠΡΑΙΤΩΡΙΩ:** Transliterated as "Praitorion" from the Greek transliteration of Latin *praetorium*. [The imperial guard headquarters or the governor's residence — the exact referent is debated.]

8. **v.14 — ΤΟΝ ΛΟΓΟΝ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ:** Sinaiticus reads "the word of God" (ΤΟΝ ΛΟΓΟΝ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ). Some manuscripts read "the word" without "of God," and others read "the word of the Lord." The Sinaiticus reading is followed.

9. **vv.16-17 — Order reversal:** In Sinaiticus, the order of vv. 16-17 appears with the love-motivated proclaimers in v.16 and the selfish-ambition proclaimers in v.17. This follows the Sinaiticus arrangement. Some later manuscripts (and the Textus Receptus) reverse these two verses, placing selfish-ambition first (as v.16) and love second (as v.17).

10. **v.23 — ΠΟΛΛΩ ΜΑΛΛΟΝ ΚΡΕΙΣΣΟΝ:** A triple comparative construction — literally "much more better." This grammatically unusual intensification is preserved as-is; Paul stacks comparative forms for emphasis.

11. **v.27 — ΠΟΛΙΤΕΥΕΣΘΕ:** Translated "conduct-yourselves-as-citizens" rather than merely "live" or "conduct yourselves." The verb ΠΟΛΙΤΕΥΟΜΑΙ carries civic overtones, significant given that Philippi was a Roman colony. Paul deliberately chose this word over a more generic term like ΠΕΡΙΠΑΤΕΩ ("walk").

12. **v.29 — ΤΟ ΥΠΕΡ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ:** The articular infinitive construction with the article ΤΟ governing both infinitives (to-believe and to-suffer) is preserved in the translation. The article makes both actions substantival — "the believing" and "the suffering" are presented as gifts granted by God.

### General Notes

- Nomina sacra are expanded throughout (e.g., ΧΥ → ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ, ΙΥ → ΙΗΣΟΥ, ΘΥ → ΘΕΟΥ, ΚΥ → ΚΥΡΙΟΥ, ΠΝΣ → ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΟΣ).
- Sinaiticus is written in scriptio continua (no word divisions); word divisions are my own parsing.
- The manuscript shows corrector activity on this page, though the primary hand (scribe A) is followed unless noted.
- Itacisms (common vowel interchange errors in Sinaiticus) have been noted where they affect meaning; purely orthographic itacisms are silently resolved.

— EMET
